title: Audit and fix Show, Faction, Title, League list views for universe filtering
description:
Verify that ShowListView, FactionListView, TitleListView, and LeagueListView consistently filter by the current universe. Fix any that don't.

## Why
These repositories already have findByUniverse methods, but the views may not be using UniverseContextService to pass the right universe in. A systematic audit is needed.

## What to implement
For each view (ShowListView, FactionListView, TitleListView, LeagueListView):
1. Locate where the Grid data is loaded (look for repository calls or service calls that fetch the list)
2. Check if UniverseContextService is injected and called
3. If not: inject UniverseContextService, call getCurrentUniverse() before loading data, pass the universe to the findByUniverse method
4. If already done: confirm it's correct and note it as verified

TitleListView special case: the title listing includes championship reigns (TitleReign) with a universe_id filter already in JPQL — verify this is correctly parameterized.

## Acceptance criteria
- Switching the universe selector causes ShowListView, FactionListView, TitleListView, and LeagueListView each to update their data
- No view shows entities from a different universe than the selected one
- Edge case: if universe returns empty Optional, fall back to universe ID=1, do not throw

title: Add CampaignRepository.findByUniverse and wire CampaignListView
description:
CampaignRepository is missing a findByUniverse method, and CampaignListView does not filter by the selected universe.

## Why
Every other major entity list (Show, Faction, Title, League) can filter by universe. Campaign is the only gap. Without this, switching universes has no effect on the campaign list.

## What to implement
- CampaignRepository: add findByUniverse(Universe universe): List<Campaign>
- CampaignListView: inject UniverseContextService; on page load (and after universe change triggers a reload), call universeContextService.getCurrentUniverse() and pass the result to findByUniverse; if universe is empty, use the default (ID=1)

## Acceptance criteria
- Switching the universe selector updates the campaign list to show only campaigns in the selected universe
- If no universe is set, the default universe campaigns are shown
- No existing campaign tests are broken

title: Add member management UI to Universe admin
description:
Extend the Universe admin UI with a member management panel so admins can add and remove accounts from a universe.

## Why
Admins need to assign users to universes through the UI. Without this, the membership table has no management interface.

## What to implement
- Option A (preferred): Add a 'Members' tab to UniverseFormDialog using Vaadin Tabs component
  - Tab 1: Basic info (name, type) — existing content
  - Tab 2: Members — shows current members in a Grid<UniverseMembership> with columns: Account username, Role (editable ComboBox), Joined date, Remove button
  - Below the grid: an Account search ComboBox + Role selector + 'Add Member' button
- The Account search ComboBox should use a lazy data provider filtering accounts by username
- Remove button calls UniverseMembershipService.removeMember and refreshes the grid
- Add Member calls UniverseMembershipService.addMember and refreshes the grid
- Show inline notification if adding a duplicate or removing a non-existent member

## Acceptance criteria
- Admins can see all members of a universe from the edit dialog
- Admins can add an account as a MEMBER or OWNER
- Admins can remove a member from the universe
- Duplicate add shows an error notification instead of throwing
- The Members tab is only visible in edit mode (not in create mode, since the universe doesn't exist yet)

title: Update MainLayout universe selector to respect membership
description:
The universe ComboBox in MainLayout currently shows all universes to everyone. Update it to show only the current user's accessible universes.

## Why
Non-admin users should only be able to switch between universes they are members of. Currently they see and can select any universe in the system.

## What to implement
- Inject SecurityUtils and UniverseMembershipService into MainLayout
- When populating the universe ComboBox:
  - If the current user has ADMIN role: universeRepository.findAll() (existing behavior)
  - Otherwise: universeMembershipService.getUniversesForAccount(currentAccount)
- After populating, check if the currently active universe (from UniverseContextService) is still in the accessible list
  - If not: call universeContextService.setCurrentUniverse(firstAvailableUniverse) or clear the context if the list is empty
  - If the list is empty: disable the ComboBox and show a placeholder 'No universes assigned'
- The page reload behavior on selection change stays the same

## Acceptance criteria
- Admin users see all universes in the ComboBox
- Non-admin users see only their assigned universes
- A user whose active universe was revoked is automatically moved to their first remaining universe (or shown the empty state)
- The empty state ('No universes assigned') is shown as disabled ComboBox, not a crash or blank

title: Create UniverseMembershipRepository and UniverseMembershipService
description:
Repository and service layer for managing universe membership.

## Why
Views and the MainLayout selector need a clean API to query a user's universes and to add/remove members.

## What to implement
- UniverseMembershipRepository extends JpaRepository<UniverseMembership, Long>
  - findByAccount(Account account): List<UniverseMembership>
  - findByUniverse(Universe universe): List<UniverseMembership>
  - findByAccountAndUniverse(Account account, Universe universe): Optional<UniverseMembership>
  - existsByAccountAndUniverse(Account account, Universe universe): boolean
- UniverseMembershipService annotated @Service
  - getUniversesForAccount(Account account): List<Universe> — returns universes the account is a member of
  - getMembersForUniverse(Universe universe): List<UniverseMembership>
  - addMember(Universe universe, Account account, UniverseMemberRole role): UniverseMembership — throws if already a member
  - removeMember(Universe universe, Account account): void — throws if not a member
  - isMember(Universe universe, Account account): boolean

## Acceptance criteria
- getUniversesForAccount returns only universes the account is explicitly a member of
- addMember and removeMember are idempotent-safe (throw clear exceptions on invalid states, not NPE)
- All repository calls are through the service in consuming code

title: Create UniverseMembership entity and Flyway migrations
description:
Add an explicit Account-Universe join entity and the corresponding DB migrations.

## Why
Without an explicit membership table, there is no clean way to determine which universes a user can access. Indirect inference through LeagueMembership is too fragile and doesn't cover users with admin roles in a universe who have no league.

## What to implement
- UniverseMembership entity in package com.github.javydreamercsw.management.domain.universe
  - @ManyToOne Account account (FK to accounts table)
  - @ManyToOne Universe universe (FK to universe table)  
  - UniverseMemberRole role enum: OWNER, MEMBER (stored as STRING)
  - @CreationTimestamp Instant joinedDate
  - Composite or surrogate PK (follow existing patterns in the codebase)
- Flyway migration V??__Add_Universe_Membership.sql in BOTH h2/ and mysql/ directories
  - CREATE TABLE universe_members (id BIGINT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY, account_id BIGINT NOT NULL, universe_id BIGINT NOT NULL, role VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL, joined_date TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP, FOREIGN KEY (account_id) REFERENCES accounts(id), FOREIGN KEY (universe_id) REFERENCES universe(id), UNIQUE KEY uk_universe_member (account_id, universe_id))
  - INSERT a row making all existing accounts members of the Default Universe (ID=1) so existing users are not locked out

## Acceptance criteria
- Migration runs cleanly on a fresh DB for both H2 and MySQL
- UniverseMembership entity is persisted correctly via JPA
- Existing accounts are automatically seeded as MEMBER of Universe ID 1 by the migration

title: Add Universe menu entry in MenuService
description:
Add 'Universes' to the navigation menu under the Entities section, visible only to admins.

## Why
Without a menu entry, the UniverseListView is only reachable by direct URL. It needs to appear in the same Entities section as Wrestlers, Titles, etc.

## What to implement
- In MenuService.java, add an entry for UniverseListView in the Entities section
- Restrict to RoleName.ADMIN (same pattern as other admin-only items like Arenas, Accounts)
- Icon: vaadin:globe or similar that conveys 'universe/world'
- Label: 'Universes'

## Acceptance criteria
- 'Universes' appears in the menu for ADMIN users
- 'Universes' does not appear for BOOKER, PLAYER, or VIEWER roles
- Clicking the menu item navigates to /universe-list

title: Create UniverseListView admin page
description:
Vaadin view listing all universes with create, edit, and delete actions.

## Why
Admins need a dedicated page to manage universes. Currently no such page exists.

## What to implement
- UniverseListView in package com.github.javydreamercsw.management.ui.view.universe
- Annotations: @Route('universe-list'), @Menu(order = X, icon = 'vaadin:globe'), @RolesAllowed(RoleName.ADMIN)
- Grid<Universe> with columns: Name, Type (display UniverseType enum label), Created (formatted date), Actions
- Actions column: Edit button (opens UniverseFormDialog in edit mode), Delete button (shows confirmation dialog)
- Delete confirmation: 'Are you sure you want to delete universe {name}? This cannot be undone.' with Confirm/Cancel
- If UniverseService.delete throws IllegalStateException, show an error notification with the exception message
- 'Create Universe' button in the header area opens UniverseFormDialog in create mode
- Grid refreshes after any create/edit/delete operation

## Acceptance criteria
- Page is accessible only to ADMIN role
- All universes appear in the grid
- Create opens the dialog and new universe appears in grid after save
- Edit pre-fills the dialog with existing data
- Delete with referenced entities shows error notification instead of deleting
- Delete empty universe asks for confirmation and removes the row on confirm

title: Create UniverseFormDialog for create/edit
description:
Vaadin dialog component for creating and editing a Universe.

## Why
Admins need a form to enter universe details. This follows the existing TitleFormDialog pattern.

## What to implement
- UniverseFormDialog extends Dialog in package com.github.javydreamercsw.management.ui.view.universe
- Constructor: UniverseFormDialog(UniverseService, Universe toEdit) — pass null for create mode
- Fields: name (TextField, required, max 255 chars), type (ComboBox<UniverseType> showing all enum values)
- Buttons: Save (calls UniverseService.save) and Cancel
- On save success: fire a ComponentEvent or call a callback so the parent list view can refresh
- On validation failure: show inline error messages using Vaadin's validation API
- Title bar: 'Create Universe' for new, 'Edit Universe: {name}' for edit

## Acceptance criteria
- Form pre-populates fields when editing an existing universe
- Name field shows error if blank or duplicate
- Save calls UniverseService.save and closes the dialog on success
- Cancel closes without saving

title: Create UniverseService with CRUD methods
description:
Implement a Spring service that wraps UniverseRepository with business logic for create, update, delete, and validation.

## Why
UniverseRepository exists but views should not call repositories directly. A service layer is needed to enforce the rule that you cannot delete a universe with active references.

## What to implement
- UniverseService class annotated @Service in package com.github.javydreamercsw.management.service.universe
- Methods: findAll(), findById(Long), findByName(String), save(Universe), delete(Long)
- delete(Long): before deleting, check ShowRepository.existsByUniverse, FactionRepository.existsByUniverse, TitleRepository, LeagueRepository, CampaignRepository — throw an IllegalStateException with a descriptive message if any return true
- save(Universe): validate that name is not blank and that no other universe with the same name exists (for creates); call universeRepository.save()

## Acceptance criteria
- Service is injectable in views
- Deleting a universe with referenced entities throws an exception with a useful message
- All repository calls go through the service, not directly from views

title: [Phase 3] Audit and fix universe filtering across all list views
description:
Ensure every entity list view filters its data by the currently selected universe from UniverseContextService.

## Why this exists
The universe selector in MainLayout sets a session-scoped universe but many views load data without filtering by it. CampaignRepository has no findByUniverse method. Other repositories have the methods but the views may not be using them consistently.

## What needs to be done

### Repository gaps
- CampaignRepository: add findByUniverse(Universe universe) method

### View audit — for each view below, verify it calls UniverseContextService.getCurrentUniverse() and passes the result to the repository findByUniverse method. Fix any that don't:
- ShowListView: should use ShowRepository.findByUniverse(universe)
- FactionListView: should use FactionRepository.findByUniverse(universe)
- TitleListView: verify universe filtering is in place for the championship lineage queries
- LeagueListView: should use LeagueRepository.findByUniverse(universe)
- CampaignListView: add universe filtering using the new CampaignRepository method

### Edge cases
- If no universe is selected (session empty), fall back to the default universe (ID=1)
- If UniverseContextService.getCurrentUniverse() returns empty, show an empty grid with a 'Select a universe' placeholder rather than throwing an exception

## Acceptance criteria
- Switching the universe selector updates the data in ShowListView, FactionListView, TitleListView, LeagueListView, and CampaignListView
- No list view shows entities from a different universe than the one selected
- Views handle an empty/unset universe gracefully

title: [Phase 2] Universe membership table and scoped selector
description:
Add an explicit Account-Universe membership model so non-admin users only see and can select universes they belong to.

## Why this exists
Currently the MainLayout ComboBox shows all universes to everyone. There is no explicit universe membership concept — the only indirect link is Account → LeagueMembership → League → Universe, which is too coarse. We need a direct membership table.

## What needs to be done
- New UniverseMembership entity: @ManyToOne Account, @ManyToOne Universe, role or permission field (e.g. OWNER, MEMBER enum)
- Flyway migrations: V??__Add_Universe_Membership.sql for both h2/ and mysql/ directories
- UniverseMembershipRepository: findByAccount(Account), findByUniverse(Universe), findByAccountAndUniverse(Account, Universe)
- UniverseMembershipService: addMember, removeMember, getUniversesForAccount, getMembersForUniverse
- Update MainLayout ComboBox: if ADMIN → universeRepository.findAll(); else → universeMembershipService.getUniversesForAccount(currentAccount)
- Handle the case where the user's current session universe is no longer in their accessible list (reset to first available or clear)
- Universe admin UI additions: Members tab in UniverseFormDialog or a UniverseMembersDialog; shows current members with role, allows adding accounts by search, allows removing members

## Acceptance criteria
- A new universe_members table exists in the DB with FK to accounts and universes
- Non-admin users see only universes they are explicitly members of in the selector
- Admins see all universes regardless of membership
- Admins can add/remove members from a universe in the universe admin UI
- If a user's active universe is revoked, their session resets gracefully

title: [Phase 1] Universe CRUD admin UI
description:
Create the full admin UI for managing universes: service layer, list view, and form dialog.

## Why this exists
There is no way for admins to create, edit, or delete universes through the application. The Universe entity and repository exist but only the MainLayout selector consumes them.

## What needs to be done
- UniverseService: thin CRUD service wrapping UniverseRepository
- UniverseListView: Vaadin Grid listing all universes with name, type, creation date, member count columns; edit and delete action buttons; ADMIN-only @RolesAllowed; @Route('universe-list')
- UniverseFormDialog: dialog component with name TextField and type ComboBox<UniverseType>; handles both create and edit; validates that name is unique
- Menu entry in MenuService under the Entities section, ADMIN role only
- Delete confirmation dialog that blocks deletion if any entities (Show, Faction, Title, League, Campaign) still reference that universe

## Acceptance criteria
- Admin can navigate to universe list from the main menu
- Admin can create a new universe with a name and type
- Admin can edit an existing universe's name and type
- Admin cannot delete a universe that has entities referencing it
- Admin can delete an empty universe with a confirmation dialog
- Non-admin users do not see the menu entry or the route

## Implementation notes
Follow the TitleListView + TitleFormDialog pattern already in the codebase. Use @RolesAllowed(RoleName.ADMIN) on the view class.

title: Universe UI: Full implementation (CRUD, membership, view filtering, wrestler settings)
description:
The Universe data and service layers are implemented but there is almost no UI. This epic covers everything needed to make Universe a first-class concept in the application: admin CRUD, explicit membership management, universe-scoped view filtering, and per-universe wrestler/expansion settings.

## Why this exists
- Users have no way to create, edit, or delete universes through the UI
- The MainLayout universe selector shows all universes to all users (should be membership-scoped)
- Most list views do not consistently filter by the selected universe
- Wrestler availability per universe is not configurable

## What needs to be done
Four phases:
1. Universe CRUD admin UI (Phase 1)
2. Explicit universe membership table + scoped selector (Phase 2)
3. View universe filtering audit and fixes (Phase 3)
4. Per-universe wrestler/expansion settings (Phase 4)

## Acceptance criteria
- Admins can create, edit, delete universes through the UI
- Non-admin users only see universes they are members of in the selector
- All entity list views (Show, Faction, Title, League, Campaign) filter by the selected universe
- Admins can configure expansion and wrestler availability per universe

title: [Phase 4] Per-universe wrestler exclusion list
description:
Allow admins to mark specific wrestlers as unavailable within a particular universe.

## Why
The user confirmed that beyond expansion-level filtering, admins should be able to disable individual wrestlers per universe. This is finer-grained than expansion toggles.

## What to implement
- UniverseWrestlerExclusion entity: @ManyToOne Universe, @ManyToOne Wrestler, reason String (optional), @CreationTimestamp excludedDate
- Flyway migrations: V??__Add_Universe_Wrestler_Exclusions.sql for both h2/ and mysql/
- UniverseWrestlerExclusionRepository: findByUniverse(Universe), findByWrestler(Wrestler), existsByUniverseAndWrestler(Universe, Wrestler)
- UniverseWrestlerExclusionService: excludeWrestler(Universe, Wrestler, String reason), includeWrestler(Universe, Wrestler), isExcluded(Universe, Wrestler), getAvailableWrestlers(Universe): filters all active wrestlers minus excluded ones
- Admin UI: an 'Excluded Wrestlers' tab in the universe admin UI with a Grid of excluded wrestlers (name, exclusion date, reason, Re-include button) and a wrestler search picker to add exclusions
- WrestlerListView and any wrestler picker components (e.g., draft picker, segment builder): call UniverseWrestlerExclusionService.getAvailableWrestlers(currentUniverse) instead of loading all wrestlers

## Acceptance criteria
- Admins can exclude a wrestler from a universe through the UI
- Excluded wrestlers do not appear in WrestlerListView when that universe is selected
- Excluded wrestlers do not appear in wrestler pickers (drafts, segment builder, etc.)
- Re-including a wrestler restores their availability immediately (on next page load)
- Exclusions are per-universe: excluding in universe A does not affect universe B

title: [Phase 4] Per-universe expansion enable/disable settings
description:
Move expansion availability toggles from global admin settings to per-universe settings.

## Why
The user confirmed that expansion enable/disable should be configurable per universe, not globally. Different universes may want different wrestler sets (eras, promotions, etc.).

## Note: blocked on discovery
This issue should NOT be started until the discovery issue (Phase 4 discovery) is complete and its notes describe the expansion model.

## Likely what to implement (update based on discovery findings)
- A UniverseExpansionSetting entity or config: (Universe, expansion identifier, enabled boolean)
- Flyway migrations for h2 and mysql
- UniverseExpansionSettingsRepository and service methods: getEnabledExpansions(Universe), setExpansionEnabled(Universe, expansion, boolean)
- Admin UI: a 'Settings' or 'Expansions' tab in the universe admin UI showing toggle switches for each expansion
- Seed: all expansions enabled for the Default Universe on migration

## Acceptance criteria (draft — refine after discovery)
- Per-universe expansion toggles are independent: enabling in universe A does not affect universe B
- The Default Universe has all expansions enabled after migration
- Admins can toggle expansions on/off per universe from the universe admin UI

title: [Phase 4 discovery] Investigate existing expansion/admin settings model
description:
Before implementing per-universe expansion settings, understand what expansions are and how the current admin settings work.

## Why
The user confirmed that the admin settings have expansion toggles that should be per-universe. We need to understand the existing model before designing the per-universe layer.

## What to investigate
- Search for 'expansion' in all entity, service, view, and migration files
- Identify what an expansion is: is it an enum, a DB table, a config file, or something else?
- Find the current admin settings UI and how expansion toggles work today
- Determine which entities (wrestlers?) are scoped by expansion
- Check if there is already any per-universe or per-league differentiation of expansion availability
- Document the findings as notes on this issue before any implementation begins

## Deliverable
Update this issue with notes describing:
- What expansion is in this codebase (class/table names, key fields)
- How expansions are currently toggled (admin settings flow)
- What a per-universe expansion settings model would look like
- Any complications or existing patterns to follow

title: [Phase 4] Per-universe wrestler and expansion settings
description:
Allow admins to configure which wrestlers and expansions are available within each universe.

## Why this exists
The user confirmed that the admin settings already include expansion toggles, and those should be per-universe rather than global. Additionally, admins need the ability to exclude individual wrestlers from a specific universe.

## Discovery required (do this first)
Before implementing, investigate the existing expansion/admin settings:
- Search for 'expansion' in the codebase (entities, services, views, DB migrations)
- Understand what an expansion is in this context (era? content pack? wrestler set?)
- Identify the current admin settings UI and how global expansion toggles work
- Determine whether the expansion concept maps to existing DB tables or is purely config

## What needs to be done (based on discovery)
Likely deliverables:
- Per-universe expansion settings: a universe_expansion_settings join table or config entity linking Universe to expansion identifiers with an enabled boolean; Flyway migrations for h2 and mysql
- Per-universe wrestler exclusion list: a universe_wrestler_exclusions table (Universe FK + Wrestler FK); Flyway migrations; UniverseWrestlerExclusionRepository
- UniverseSettingsService: methods to enable/disable expansions per universe, exclude/include wrestlers per universe
- Universe settings admin UI: a Settings tab within the universe admin UI (Phase 1) with expansion toggles and a wrestler exclusion picker
- WrestlerListView or wrestler picker components: filter out excluded wrestlers when the current universe has them excluded

## Acceptance criteria
- Admins can enable/disable each expansion independently per universe
- Admins can mark specific wrestlers as unavailable in a universe
- Wrestler list views and picker components respect per-universe exclusions
- Per-universe settings are independent (disabling an expansion in universe A does not affect universe B)

title: Verify end-to-end: scan QR with phone
description: Manual verification: start the app, navigate to a show detail view, confirm QR icon appears on match rows but not promo rows, click QR icon, confirm dialog shows QR code and URL. Navigate directly to MatchView and confirm Share QR Code button appears. Scan QR with a phone and confirm it opens the correct match URL. Part of EPIC ATW-131.

title: Add QR button to MatchView header
description: Add a Share QR Code button (VaadinIcon.QRCODE) to the MatchView header/toolbar. Clicking opens QrCodeDialog with the current matchId from the :matchId route parameter. Part of EPIC ATW-131.

title: Add QR button to ShowDetailView segment grid rows
description: Add a VaadinIcon.QRCODE icon button to the actions column of the segment grid in ShowDetailView. Visible only for match-type segments (not promos). Clicking opens QrCodeDialog with the segment's match ID. Part of EPIC ATW-131.

title: Create QrCodeDialog Vaadin component
description: Create QrCodeDialog.java in management/ui/view/match extending Vaadin Dialog. Constructor takes matchId, builds full URL via UrlUtil.getBaseUrl(), generates QR via QrCodeUtil, displays as Vaadin Image (data:image/png;base64,...) with URL text below. Mirrors GenericImageGenerationDialog pattern. Part of EPIC ATW-131.

title: Create QrCodeUtil server-side generation utility
description: Create QrCodeUtil.java in management/util package with a toBase64Png(String content, int size) method using ZXing to generate a QR code returned as a base64-encoded PNG string. Reuses UrlUtil.getBaseUrl() to build match URLs. Part of EPIC ATW-131.

title: Add ZXing dependencies to pom.xml
description: Add com.google.zxing:core and com.google.zxing:javase (v3.5.3) to pom.xml to enable server-side QR code generation. Part of EPIC ATW-131.

title: EPIC: QR code share button for match URL
description: Bookers need a quick way to share the MatchView URL with players during live sessions. This epic adds a QR code feature: a button on ShowDetailView match segment rows and in the MatchView header that opens a dialog containing a scannable QR code encoding the full match URL. Players scan with their phones to go directly to the match result page.

title: Verify end-to-end: scan QR with phone
description: Manual verification: start the app, navigate to a show detail view, confirm QR icon appears on match rows but not promo rows, click QR icon, confirm dialog shows QR code + URL, navigate to MatchView directly and confirm Share QR Code button appears. Scan QR with a phone and confirm it opens the correct match URL. Part of EPIC ATW-msm.

title: Add QR button to MatchView header
description: Add a Share QR Code button (with VaadinIcon.QRCODE) to the MatchView header/toolbar area. Clicking opens QrCodeDialog with the current matchId (already available from the :matchId route parameter). Part of EPIC ATW-msm.

title: Add QR button to ShowDetailView segment grid rows
description: Add a VaadinIcon.QRCODE icon button to the actions column of the segment grid in ShowDetailView. Button should only be visible for match-type segments (not promos). Clicking opens QrCodeDialog with the segment's match ID. Part of EPIC ATW-msm.

title: Create QrCodeDialog Vaadin component
description: Create QrCodeDialog.java in management/ui/view/match package extending Vaadin Dialog. Constructor takes matchId, builds the full URL via UrlUtil.getBaseUrl(), generates QR via QrCodeUtil, displays it as a Vaadin Image (data:image/png;base64,...) with the URL text below. Mirrors GenericImageGenerationDialog pattern. Part of EPIC ATW-msm.

title: Create QrCodeUtil server-side generation utility
description: Create QrCodeUtil.java in management/util package with a toBase64Png(String content, int size) method that uses ZXing to generate a QR code and returns it as a base64-encoded PNG string. Reuses UrlUtil.getBaseUrl() to build match URLs. Part of EPIC ATW-msm.

title: Add ZXing dependencies to pom.xml
description: Add com.google.zxing:core and com.google.zxing:javase (v3.5.3) to pom.xml to enable server-side QR code generation. Part of EPIC ATW-msm.

title: EPIC: QR code share button for match URL
description: Bookers need a quick way to share the MatchView URL with players during live sessions. This epic adds a QR code feature: a button on ShowDetailView match segment rows and in the MatchView header that opens a dialog containing a scannable QR code encoding the full match URL. Players scan with their phones to go directly to the match result page.

title: Implement modifyAttribute temporary/permanent buffs
description: CampaignEffectContext.modifyAttribute() is a stub. Implement attribute modification (e.g. brawl, charisma, resilience) that can be applied temporarily (match-scoped) or permanently via WrestlerState.

title: Implement modifyRoll/modifyOpponentRoll temporary modifiers
description: CampaignEffectContext.modifyRoll() and modifyOpponentRoll() are stubs. Implement a temporary modifier system in the Match Engine that applies a one-roll bonus or penalty for the player or opponent.

title: Implement modifyBackstageDice bonus dice
description: CampaignEffectContext.modifyBackstageDice() is a stub. Integrate with BackstageActionService to add bonus dice to the next backstage check for the active wrestler.

title: Implement gainInitiative in Match Engine
description: CampaignEffectContext.gainInitiative() is a stub. Implement initiative assignment in the Match Engine so a script can transfer initiative to the player who played the card.

title: Implement negateAttack in Match Engine
description: CampaignEffectContext.negateAttack() is a stub. Implement attack cancellation in the Match Engine so an incoming attack can be voided by a script.

title: Implement pin/breakPin in Match Engine
description: CampaignEffectContext.pin() and breakPin() are stubs. Wire pin attempt and forced pin-break logic into the Match Engine's match flow.

title: Wire drawCard to Deck/Hand management
description: CampaignEffectContext.drawCard() is a stub. Integrate with the Deck and Hand management system to draw the specified number of cards for the active wrestler.

title: Wire gainMomentum to Match Engine momentum tracker
description: CampaignEffectContext.gainMomentum() is a stub. Integrate with the Match Engine's momentum tracker to add momentum for the active wrestler.

title: Wire spendStamina/gainStamina to Match Engine
description: CampaignEffectContext.spendStamina() and gainStamina() are stubs. Integrate both with the Match Engine to read and write the current stamina value for the active wrestler during a match.

title: Wire gainHitPoints/damage to Match Engine
description: CampaignEffectContext.gainHitPoints() and damage() are stubs. Integrate with the Match Engine to apply healing and damage to the active wrestler and opponent respectively.

title: Implement CampaignEffectContext integrations
description: CampaignEffectContext exposes a Groovy scripting API for Campaign Ability Cards but all methods are stubs. This epic covers wiring each method to the Match Engine, Deck/Hand management, and modifier systems so campaign scripts have real game effect.

title: Phase 5: Final cleanup and full verification of deprecated method removal
description:
After Phases 1-4, all 27 deprecated methods should be deleted. Final cleanup pass and full E2E test run to confirm nothing regressed.

**Why:** This is the gate phase that proves the entire refactor is safe before merging.

**Steps:**
1. Check if getDefaultState() is still referenced in Wrestler.java — if Phases 1-4 are complete it should have zero callers and can be deleted too.

2. Verify no @Deprecated annotations remain on Wrestler:
```bash
grep -n '@Deprecated' src/main/java/com/github/javydreamercsw/management/domain/wrestler/Wrestler.java
# must return zero results
```

3. Run full build:
```bash
mvn spotless:apply
mvn clean verify -Pe2e,coverage,production
```

4. Run targeted grep for any surviving deprecated callers:
```bash
grep -rn 'wrestler\.' src/main/ --include='*.java' | grep -E '(getTier|getBumps|getFans|setTier|setBumps|setCurrentHealth|getActiveInjuries|getTotalInjuryPenalty|getCurrentHealthWithPenalties|getFanWeight|addFans|canAfford|addBump|spendFans|refreshCurrentHealth|getFaction|setFaction|getManager|setManager|getPhysicalCondition|setPhysicalCondition|getMorale|setMorale|getManagementStamina|setManagementStamina|getInjuries)\(\)' | grep -v WrestlerState | grep -v WrestlerService
# must return zero results
```

5. Create beads issues for any E2E regressions discovered.

6. Push all commits:
```bash
git pull --rebase
bd dolt push
git push
git status  # must show 'up to date with origin'
```

**Acceptance criteria:** mvn clean verify passes with zero deprecated method callers remaining on Wrestler entity.

title: Phase 4: Fix production UI callers of deprecated Wrestler methods
description:
6 deprecated methods are called in production UI/service code. Each needs a universeId-aware replacement. Fix all callers, then delete the deprecated methods.

**Why:** UI components call deprecated getters on Wrestler that route through getDefaultState() instead of using the correct universe-scoped WrestlerState. This must be refactored before the stubs can be deleted.

**4A: getFanWeight() in ShowService (line 636)**
File: src/main/java/com/github/javydreamercsw/management/service/show/ShowService.java
- Replace wrestler.getFanWeight() with wrestler.getFanWeight(universeId)
- A non-deprecated overload getFanWeight(universeId) already exists on Wrestler
- Find universeId from the Show entity or UniverseContextService

**4B: getTier() across 9 locations**
Files + lines:
- PlayerCampaignCard.java:73
- RankingView.java:113
- TitleFormDialog.java:105
- NarrationDialog.java:506, 618
- DraftView.java:204
- GmDashboardView.java:92
- ShowPlanningAiService.java:275
- BackstageEncounterService.java:206
- CampaignEncounterService.java:308
Pattern: Replace wrestler.getTier() with wrestlerService.getOrCreateState(wrestler.getId(), universeId).getTier(). If universeId not in scope, inject UniverseContextService.

**4C: getBumps() in UI**
Files: PlayerCampaignCard.java:162,167; BackstageActionView.java:198
- Replace wrestler.getBumps() with state.getBumps() (WrestlerState already available or fetch it)

**4D: getActiveInjuries() in UI**
Files: PlayerCampaignCard.java:181; BackstageActionView.java:198
- Replace with: injuryService.getActiveInjuriesForWrestler(wrestler.getId(), universeId)
- Inject InjuryService if not already present

**4E: getCurrentHealthWithPenalties() in PlayerCampaignCard (lines 104, 105)**
- Replace with inline calculation:
  int totalInjuryPenalty = injuryService.getAllInjuriesForWrestler(wrestler.getId(), universeId)
      .stream().filter(i -> i.isCurrentlyActive()).mapToInt(Injury::getHealthPenalty).sum();
  int effectiveHp = Math.max(1, state.getCurrentHealth() - state.getBumps() - totalInjuryPenalty);

**4F: getTotalInjuryPenalty() in WrestlerSummaryCard (line 177)**
- WrestlerSummaryCard already loads injuries via injuryService.getAllInjuriesForWrestler()
- Replace wrestlerWithDetails.getTotalInjuryPenalty() with:
  injuries.stream().filter(Injury::isCurrentlyActive).mapToInt(Injury::getHealthPenalty).sum()

**After all callers updated, delete from Wrestler.java:**
- getTier() (~line 425)
- getBumps() (~line 439)
- getActiveInjuries() (~line 535)
- getTotalInjuryPenalty() (~line 541)
- getCurrentHealthWithPenalties() (~line 578)
- getFanWeight() (~line 584) [no-arg version only]

**Verification checklist:**
```bash
mvn spotless:apply
mvn test -Dtest='!*E2ETest'
grep -r '.getTier()\|.getBumps()\|.getActiveInjuries()\|.getTotalInjuryPenalty()\|.getCurrentHealthWithPenalties()\|.getFanWeight()' src/main/ --include='*.java' | grep -v 'WrestlerState\|WrestlerService'
# ^ must return zero results
```
notes: Additional production callers found during ATW-cz3: (7) setTier() — TierRecalculationService.java:175,208 (via WrestlerData interface), DataInitializer.java:962 (no-op on unsaved Wrestler — safe to just remove that line); WrestlerData interface itself declares setTier() at line 27 — must be removed from interface and TierRecalculationService refactored to cast to WrestlerState before calling setTier. (8) setBumps() — CampaignDashboardView.java:1084,1092 in addDebugSection() — migrate to get WrestlerState via WrestlerService.getOrCreateState() and call state.setBumps(). (9) getInjuries() — WrestlerNotionSyncService.java:127,129 — migrate to InjuryService.getAllInjuriesForWrestler(entity.getId(), universeId) or iterate wrestler states.

title: Phase 3: Refactor WrestlerService self-referential deprecated calls
description:
Three deprecated methods (getFans, addFans, canAfford) are called from within WrestlerService.java itself inside awardFans(). Fix the service to work directly against WrestlerState, then delete the deprecated stubs.

**Why:** WrestlerService.awardFans() calls deprecated methods on the Wrestler entity instead of operating on the WrestlerState it already has in hand. This is a self-referential loop through the deprecated layer.

**File:** src/main/java/com/github/javydreamercsw/management/service/wrestler/WrestlerService.java

**Goal for awardFans() (~lines 209-224):**
- The method already calls getOrCreateState(wrestlerId, universeId) — use that state variable
- Replace wrestler.canAfford(cost) with state.canAfford(cost)
- Replace wrestler.addFans(n) with state.setFans(Math.max(0, state.getFans() + n)) then save state
- Replace wrestler.getFans() with state.getFans()
- Ensure state is saved via wrestlerStateRepository.saveAndFlush(state) after mutation

**After fixing, delete from Wrestler.java:**
- getFans() (~line 417)
- addFans(long fanGain) (~line 546)
- canAfford(Long cost) (~line 556)

**Verification checklist:**
```bash
mvn spotless:apply
mvn test -Dtest='WrestlerService*,WrestlerIT*'
grep -r 'wrestler\.getFans()\|wrestler\.addFans(\|wrestler\.canAfford(' src/main/ --include='*.java'
# ^ must return zero results
```

**Anti-patterns:** Do NOT use wrestler.getDefaultState() — access state only through getOrCreateState(id, universeId).
close_reason: Deleted dead 2-arg awardFans from WrestlerService; migrated test addFans callers to state.setFans(); deleted Wrestler.addFans() and canAfford()

title: Phase 2: Migrate test-only deprecated Wrestler setters to WrestlerState
description:
5 deprecated methods have callers only in test files. Update each test to use WrestlerState directly, then delete the deprecated methods.

**Why:** Tests are the last place using setTier/setBumps/setCurrentHealth on the Wrestler entity. Once tests use WrestlerState, these stubs can be deleted.

**Files to update:**

src/test/java/com/github/javydreamercsw/TestUtils.java (line 113)
- Replace wrestler.setTier(tier) with:
  WrestlerState state = wrestlerService.getOrCreateState(wrestler.getId(), 1L);
  state.setTier(tier);
  wrestlerStateRepository.saveAndFlush(state);

src/test/java/com/github/javydreamercsw/management/controller/rivalry/RivalryControllerIT.java (lines 267, 271, 273)
- Replace wrestler.setCurrentHealth(...), wrestler.setBumps(...), wrestler.setTier(...) with WrestlerState equivalents.

src/test/java/com/github/javydreamercsw/management/controller/injury/InjuryControllerIT.java (lines 142, 146, 148)
- Replace setCurrentHealth, setBumps, setTier on Wrestler with WrestlerState setters.

src/test/java/com/github/javydreamercsw/management/controller/title/TitleControllerIT.java (line 175)
- Replace wrestler.setCurrentHealth(...).

src/test/java/com/github/javydreamercsw/management/domain/wrestler/WrestlerHealthCalculationTest.java (line 49)
- Replace wrestler.setCurrentHealth(...).

src/test/java/com/github/javydreamercsw/management/domain/show/segment/SegmentSerializationTest.java (lines 56, 62, 63, 73)
- Replace setFaction, setBumps, setTier, getInjuries() on Wrestler with WrestlerState equivalents. For getInjuries() — WrestlerState.getInjuries() always returns empty; add injury via InjuryService or InjuryRepository instead.

**After all test updates pass, delete from Wrestler.java:**
- setTier(WrestlerTier tier) (~line 432)
- setBumps(Integer bumps) (~line 445)
- setCurrentHealth(Integer currentHealth) (~line 497)
- getInjuries() (~line 529)

**Verification checklist:**
```bash
mvn spotless:apply
mvn test -Dtest='RivalryControllerIT,InjuryControllerIT,TitleControllerIT,WrestlerHealthCalculationTest,SegmentSerializationTest'
grep -r '.setTier(\|.setBumps(\|.setCurrentHealth(\|.getInjuries()' src/ --include='*.java' | grep -v 'WrestlerState\|WrestlerService\|Wrestler.java'
# ^ must return zero results
```
notes: Phase 1 analysis found additional setFaction callers on Wrestler not in original description: SegmentSerializationTest.java:56 (wrestler.setFaction(faction)), AdvancedCampaignE2ETest.java:131 (player.setFaction(factions.get(0))), CampaignAdvancedChaptersE2ETest.java:203 (player.setFaction(faction)). These test callers must be migrated to use WrestlerState.setFaction() before setFaction() can be deleted from Wrestler.java.
close_reason: Migrated all test callers. Removed no-op setCurrentHealth/setBumps/setTier/setFaction calls from RivalryControllerIT, InjuryControllerIT, TitleControllerIT, WrestlerHealthCalculationTest, TestUtils, SegmentSerializationTest, TeamDTOTest. Removed vestigial setFaction setup from AdvancedCampaignE2ETest and CampaignAdvancedChaptersE2ETest (chapter forced directly). Deleted setFaction and setCurrentHealth from Wrestler.java. setTier/setBumps/getInjuries restored — production callers now documented in ATW-zm5.

title: Phase 1: Delete 14 zero-caller deprecated Wrestler methods
description:
Delete 14 deprecated methods from Wrestler.java that have zero callers anywhere in src/. Safe immediate deletion — no compile errors, no runtime changes.

**Why:** These methods have no callers. Deleting them removes misleading stubs with no refactoring cost.

**File to edit:** src/main/java/com/github/javydreamercsw/management/domain/wrestler/Wrestler.java

**Delete these methods (all @Deprecated @Transient):**
1. getFaction() (~line 452)
2. setFaction(Faction faction) (~line 458)
3. getManager() (~line 465)
4. setManager(Npc manager) (~line 471)
5. getPhysicalCondition() (~line 478)
6. setPhysicalCondition(Integer physicalCondition) (~line 484)
7. getCurrentHealth() (~line 491)
8. getMorale() (~line 504)
9. setMorale(Integer morale) (~line 510)
10. getManagementStamina() (~line 517)
11. setManagementStamina(Integer managementStamina) (~line 523)
12. addBump() (~line 551)
13. spendFans(Long cost) (~line 561)
14. refreshCurrentHealth() (~line 570)

**Verification checklist:**
```bash
mvn spotless:apply
mvn test -Dtest='!*E2ETest'
grep -r '.getFaction()\|.setFaction(\|.getManager()\|.setManager(\|.getPhysicalCondition(\|.setPhysicalCondition(\|.getMorale(\|.setMorale(\|.getManagementStamina(\|.setManagementStamina(\|.addBump(\|.spendFans(\|.refreshCurrentHealth(' src/ --include='*.java'
# ^ must return zero results
```

**Anti-patterns:** Do NOT remove getDefaultState() yet — it is still used by remaining deprecated methods in later phases.
notes: Analysis found only 6 of 14 methods are truly zero-caller: setPhysicalCondition, setMorale, setManagementStamina, addBump, spendFans, refreshCurrentHealth. The other 8 have active callers: getFaction (EditSegmentDialog, BackstageEncounterService, CampaignEncounterService, CampaignFactionService, WrestlerNotionSyncService), setFaction (SegmentSerializationTest, AdvancedCampaignE2ETest, CampaignAdvancedChaptersE2ETest), getManager (MatchView, WrestlerNotionSyncService), setManager (DataInitializer:970), getPhysicalCondition (WrestlerSummaryCard, PlayerView), getMorale (GmDashboardView), getManagementStamina (GmDashboardView), getCurrentHealth (called internally by getCurrentHealthWithPenalties which has callers in PlayerCampaignCard). canAfford was also kept - has caller in WrestlerService:209. Deleted only the 6 safe methods.
close_reason: Deleted 6 zero-caller methods: setPhysicalCondition, setMorale, setManagementStamina, addBump, spendFans, refreshCurrentHealth. Remaining 8 methods (getFaction, setFaction, getManager, setManager, getPhysicalCondition, getMorale, getManagementStamina, getCurrentHealth) have active callers catalogued in ATW-cz3 (test callers) and ATW-zm5 (production callers). canAfford kept — has caller in WrestlerService:209.

title: EPIC: Remove all deprecated Wrestler entity methods in favor of WrestlerState
description:
The Wrestler entity has 27 deprecated facade methods that delegate to WrestlerState via getDefaultState(). These stubs mislead callers into bypassing universe-aware state and must be removed.

**Why:** The codebase migrated from single-universe to multi-universe (Wrestler + WrestlerState per universeId). Deprecated methods used getDefaultState() which is fragile and non-deterministic in multi-universe contexts. The InjuryService fix (May 2026) confirmed the pattern: real state lives in WrestlerState, accessed via WrestlerService.

**How to apply:** Execute child phase issues in order. Each phase is self-contained with its own verification checklist. Recommended order: Phase 1 → Phase 3 → Phase 2 → Phase 4A-F → Phase 5.

**Key API reference:**
- wrestler.getTier() → wrestlerService.getOrCreateState(id, universeId).getTier()
- wrestler.getBumps() → state.getBumps()
- wrestler.getFans() → state.getFans()
- wrestler.getActiveInjuries() → injuryService.getActiveInjuriesForWrestler(id, universeId)
- wrestler.getFanWeight() → wrestler.getFanWeight(universeId) [non-deprecated overload exists]
- wrestler.canAfford(cost) → state.canAfford(cost)
